query: EU proposal €2 flat fee small parcels Temu Shein status vote 2025
a_issues: Content A is largely off-target for the 2025 status/vote question. Its main result is a December 2025 Consilium page about a different measure: a €3 customs duty from July 2026, explicitly distinct from the proposed €2 handling fee. It does not directly answer the status of the 2025 €2 proposal or any 2025 vote. The second result is just a Reuters sitemap and useless. This could mislead an LLM into conflating the later €3 duty with the earlier €2 handling-fee proposal.
b_issues: Content B includes some lower-quality/noisy results (Facebook, Reddit) and later-dated items about a €3 customs charge in 2026 that could confuse the issue. However, it also includes a strong Reuters result directly stating that on May 21, 2025 the EU was set to propose a €2 handling fee for low-value parcels, paid by retailers like Temu/Shein, matching the core facts in the anchor. It still does not clearly provide the later 2025 vote status.
reasoning: B better enables a correct answer because it contains the key Reuters report directly about the 2025 €2 flat/handling fee proposal for small parcels linked to Temu and Shein. That is the central fact pattern in the query and aligns with the anchor. A, by contrast, mainly provides a later December 2025/2026 €3 customs-duty measure and even says it is distinct from the €2 handling fee under discussion, so it is less useful and more likely to cause an incorrect answer. Neither set cleanly provides the full 'status vote 2025' detail, but B is clearly closer and more accurate for the queried proposal.
